name: Muffins Basic and Variations
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C).
2. Grease 12 2 1/2-inch muffin cups.
3. In a medium-sized bowl, sift together flour, baking powder, sugar, and salt.
4. In a small bowl, beat eggs with a fork. Add milk and oil; stir to combine.
5. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined—batter will be lumpy.
6. Fill each prepared muffin cup about half to two-thirds full with batter using a tablespoon.
7. Bake for 15 to 20 minutes, or until golden brown. Remove from pan and serve hot with butter, jam, or marmalade.
VARIATIONS:
GINGER MUFFINS: Add 1/2 cup finely diced candied ginger to the flour mixture before adding liquid.
BANANA PECAN MUFFINS: Use only 1/2 cup milk. Add 1/2 cup chopped pecans and 1/4 teaspoon ground nutmeg to the sifted flour. Add 1 cup mashed, peeled banana with the egg, milk, and oil.
BLUEBERRY MUFFINS: Toss 1 cup washed and well-drained fresh or frozen blueberries with the flour mixture before adding liquid.
ORANGE MUFFINS: Cut 2 peeled navel oranges into sections. Place an orange section on top of each muffin after filling it halfway; sprinkle lightly with granulated sugar.
CHEESE MUFFINS: Fold 1/2 cup grated sharp yellow cheese into the batter with the last few strokes.
SURPRISE MUFFINS: Fill cups about one-third full, drop in a teaspoon of your favorite jelly, then fill to two-thirds full. Kids love these.
COCONUT MUFFINS: Add 1 cup shredded coconut during the last few strokes of mixing. Serve as a snack with butter and milk.
CHIVE MUFFINS: Fold 1/4 cup chopped chives into the batter during the last few strokes; serve at dinner with a steak and salad.
